list.name: project
list.label: delegate.project
list.description: Repo filter (span.delegate.project = git repo basename). Default .* matches all repos; type an exact name like delegate-local, or a regex like delegate-local|grafana-tempo-local to include worktrees.

panels.title: Exit-status breakdown
panels.description: Count of delegate.sh spans over time grouped by delegate.exit_status. Status 0 is success; status 1 is a generic failure (model error, transport error); status 2 is an unsubstituted-placeholder error from the --recipe loader; status 3 is the pre-flight canary timeout (the resolved model did not respond to a 1-token probe within DELEGATE_PREFLIGHT_TIMEOUT). The series legend keys off the literal integer attribute so a new exit code surfaces automatically.

panels.title: Canary timeout rate (exit 3)
panels.description: Share of delegate.sh spans with exit_status=3 over the selected range. exit_status=3 means the pre-flight 1-token canary timed out before the full templated request was sent — typically a cold-load on a 35B-class prose-tier model, an unreachable backend, or a host where the resolved model is too large for the available memory. Track the trend rather than the absolute value: a slow drift up suggests the resolved-model preference list needs to step down a size class.

panels.title: Failed-export count
panels.description: Count of delegate.sh spans where the span status is ERROR. The exporter sets status ERROR when delegate.exit_status != 0, so this stat is the OTel-status-coded view of the exit-status timeseries above. Backends that filter on span status (rather than attribute equality) use this stat as the primary failure surface.

panels.title: Recipe-substitution failures (exit 2)
panels.description: Count of delegate.sh spans with exit_status=2. This is the --recipe loader refusing to send a partly-substituted template to the model: a missing --var key, an unknown placeholder, or a typoed recipe name. A non-zero count here means a caller is invoking a recipe wrong and getting an error rather than a degraded answer — which is the recipe loader behaving as designed.

panels.title: Recent failed spans (status=ERROR)
panels.description: Per-span breakdown of the failures in the time range — model, tier, recipe (when present), exit_status, and char counts. The char counts let an investigator correlate a stall or canary timeout to input size without leaving the dashboard. No content fields are present per ADR 0007's no-content rule.

targets.query: {resource.service.name="delegate-local" && span.delegate.project=~"${project}" && status=error} | select(span.gen_ai.request.model, span.gen_ai.provider.name, span.delegate.tier, span.delegate.recipe, span.delegate.exit_status, span.delegate.prompt_chars, span.delegate.context_chars, span.delegate.output_chars)
